items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its major infrastructure and development spending for the next fiscal year. This study session determines what public projects—from roads to parks to facilities—will get funded.

items.headline: City Council to vote on new tenant protections ordinance
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program through a municipal code amendment. This could affect renters' rights and landlord-tenant relationships in the city.

items.headline: City releases annual report on youth intervention and summer safety programs
items.summary: San José presented status updates on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including reports on the Safe Summer Initiative, Youth Empowerment Alliance, and youth intervention services. These programs support local youth safety and development.

items.headline: Downtown historic building may get expanded renovation incentive
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow developers to add 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area if they rehabilitate the building and address seismic safety. This could enable a more substantial renovation or expansion project.

items.headline: Figure AI expands San José office footprint
items.summary: Figure AI, an AI robotics startup, is investing $4.1M in a second-floor office tenant improvement at 4000 N 1st Street. This represents the company's growing presence in San José's tech ecosystem and suggests confidence in the local AI/robotics sector.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial use to a planned community with an 8-story building containing 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a major land use change for that corridor.

items.headline: Project at 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. The project will require a new public notice before the hearing is held.

items.headline: Major tech tenant improvement at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4M commercial project is underway at 1768 Automation Parkway, suggesting a significant tech company expansion or new tenant arrival in San José's growing innovation corridor. This is one of the largest permitted projects in this batch, indicating substantial corporate investment in the area.
